architecture_decisions.decision: Use Playwright for E2E testing with TypeScript
architecture_decisions.rationale: Already configured in the project, supports cross-browser testing (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit), mobile emulation, and has excellent TypeScript support

architecture_decisions.decision: Page Object Model (POM) pattern with fixture-based helpers
architecture_decisions.rationale: Existing helpers.ts provides a solid foundation; POM improves maintainability by separating test logic from page interaction logic
Tests should not depend on backend services; mocking ensures consistent test data and faster test execution

architecture_decisions.decision: Visual regression testing using Playwright's built-in screenshot comparison
architecture_decisions.rationale: Ensures UI consistency across browsers and detects unintended visual changes; Playwright has native support for screenshots
Generates realistic test data on-demand; avoids hardcoded data and enables parallel test execution without conflicts
Supports testing against local, staging, and production-like environments with different base URLs and mock configurations

architecture_decisions.decision: Real-time update testing with WebSocket mocking
architecture_decisions.rationale: Tests notification-service WebSocket behavior; critical for print job status updates and agent heartbeat monitoring
Tests for Secure Print Release, Cost Allocation, Print Policy Engine, Microsoft 365 Integration, and Compliance features validate competitive parity

backend_tasks.purpose: Microsoft 365 OIDC provider handler for E2E test mocking support
New microservice for FedRAMP and HIPAA compliance tracking
Enhanced policy engine for E2E testable rule evaluation
Add WebSocket endpoint for real-time agent status updates

Settings page object for profile, security, and preferences

Cost tracking and quotas management page object
Print policy engine management page object
Microsoft 365 integration configuration and testing page object
Compliance reports and audit log export page object
Factory for generating realistic test data (users, jobs, printers, policies)
Visual regression tests using Playwright screenshot comparison
WebSocket mock utilities for testing real-time updates
Microsoft 365 integration E2E tests (OneDrive, SharePoint, authentication)
FedRAMP and HIPAA compliance testing (audit logs, data export, security)
WebSocket real-time update tests (job status, agent heartbeat, notifications)
Visual regression tests across browsers and viewport sizes
Mobile responsiveness tests for all major pages
Performance metrics tests (page load, interaction timing, bundle size)
Extend with additional mock data and helper functions for new features
Add projects for visual regression and configure snapshot options
Accessibility testing (WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation)
Frontend UI page for Microsoft 365 integration configuration
Frontend UI page for compliance reports and audit export

database_migrations.sql:
CREATE TABLE compliance_audit_logs (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  event_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  resource_type VARCHAR(50),
  resource_id UUID,
  ip_address INET,
  user_agent TEXT,
  compliance_framework VARCHAR(20) CHECK (compliance_framework IN ('HIPAA', 'FedRAMP', 'SOC2', 'GDPR')),
  data_classification VARCHAR(20) CHECK (data_classification IN ('PHI', 'PII', 'PUBLIC', 'INTERNAL')),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  INDEX idx_compliance_org (org_id),
  INDEX idx_compliance_framework (compliance_framework),
  INDEX idx_compliance_created (created_at)
);

CREATE TABLE print_release_pins (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
  pin_hash VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  card_id VARCHAR(255),
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  expires_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  last_used_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE,
  INDEX idx_release_user (user_id),
  INDEX idx_release_active (is_active)
);

CREATE TABLE quota_allocations (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  department_id UUID,
  quota_type VARCHAR(20) CHECK (quota_type IN ('monthly_pages', 'monthly_cost', 'color_pages')),
  limit_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
  used_value INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  period_type VARCHAR(10) CHECK (period_type IN ('monthly', 'quarterly', 'annual')),
  action_on_exceed VARCHAR(20) CHECK (action_on_exceed IN ('block', 'warn', 'charge')),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (org_id, user_id, quota_type, period_type),
  INDEX idx_quota_org (org_id),
  INDEX idx_quota_user (user_id)
);

CREATE TABLE print_policies (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  priority INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  conditions JSONB NOT NULL,
  actions JSONB NOT NULL,
  created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  INDEX idx_policy_org (org_id),
  INDEX idx_policy_active (is_active),
  INDEX idx_policy_priority (priority)
);

CREATE TABLE microsoft365_config (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  tenant_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  client_id VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  client_secret_encrypted TEXT,
  is_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  onedrive_enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  sharepoint_site_url TEXT,
  default_document_library VARCHAR(255),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  UNIQUE (org_id),
  INDEX idx_m365_org (org_id)
);

CREATE TABLE m365_document_mappings (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  org_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES organizations(id),
  user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  job_id UUID REFERENCES print_jobs(id),
  m365_drive_id VARCHAR(255),
  m365_item_id VARCHAR(500),
  item_url TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  INDEX idx_m365_doc_org (org_id),
  INDEX idx_m365_doc_job (job_id)
);

CREATE TABLE agent_heartbeats (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  agent_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES agents(id),
  status VARCHAR(20) CHECK (status IN ('online', 'offline', 'error')),
  job_queue_depth INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  memory_usage INTEGER,
  cpu_usage FLOAT,
  ip_address INET,
  session_state VARCHAR(20),
  received_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
  INDEX idx_heartbeat_agent (agent_id),
  INDEX idx_heartbeat_received (received_at)
);

e2e_tests.name: Authentication Flow Tests
steps: Verify login form elements are visible

e2e_tests.name: Microsoft 365 Integration Tests
steps: Verify print job creation with OneDrive document

e2e_tests.name: Secure Print Release Tests
steps: Verify job is released to printer
Verify job is removed from queue after release

e2e_tests.name: Quota Management Tests
steps: Set limit and action on exceed

e2e_tests.name: Print Policy Engine Tests
steps: Verify policy is applied to matching jobs

e2e_tests.name: Compliance Audit Tests
steps: Verify report includes all required fields

e2e_tests.name: Mobile Responsiveness Tests
steps: Verify tap targets are properly spaced

e2e_tests.name: Accessibility Tests
steps: Run axe-core accessibility scan on each page
Verify all images have alt text
Verify ARIA attributes on interactive elements
Test keyboard navigation through all features
Verify color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA
Test tab order follows logical flow

e2e_tests.name: Performance Tests
steps: Verify bundle sizes are within limits
Test lazy loading of images and components

security_notes: All E2E tests must use mock credentials - never use production credentials
API tokens in tests should be clearly marked as test tokens with limited scope
Screenshot tests must not capture sensitive user data in test runs
PII/PHI data in compliance tests must use anonymized fixtures
Print release PINs in tests should use predictable values for automation
Microsoft 365 integration tests should use dedicated test tenant
WebSocket connections in tests must point to test environment only
Audit log export tests should validate data redaction for PII
Rate limiting tests should use separate test IP ranges
Session tokens should have short expiry in test environments
docker_changes: Add E2E test runner service to docker-compose.yml
Configure test environment variables in docker-compose.test.yml
Add volume for test screenshots and reports
Add mock API server container for E2E test isolation
market_driven_features: Secure Print Release System - Tests cover PIN-based and card-based job release at print stations
Cost Allocation & Quotas - Tests for per-user, per-department quotas with blocking/warning actions
Print Policy Engine - Tests for rule-based print policies with priority evaluation
Microsoft 365 Integration - Tests for OneDrive/SharePoint document printing and OIDC authentication
FedRAMP & HIPAA Compliance - Tests for audit logging, data classification, and compliance reporting
Real-time Updates - WebSocket tests for job status, agent heartbeat, and notifications
Mobile Responsiveness - Cross-device tests for iOS/Android viewport compatibility
Visual Regression - Screenshot comparison tests for UI consistency across browsers
Accessibility Testing - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance tests for inclusive design
